author: @aurelianobuendia1178
text:
Hi again, I have understood that after obtaining graphene oxide, must aplly it ultrasonic irradiation to exfoliate the layers of graphene oxide. I found a paper where chemically modified graphene (CMG) was obtained by reduction of GO with ascorbic acid (vitamin C) whose final product, CMG has a conductivity of 3160 S / m. I have evaluated the possibility of use it process for  graphite oxide and then apply  ultrasound, I think that the final product would have higher purity. If you are interested in reviewing the paper: ingenierias.uanl.mx/52/52_Obtencion.pdf

Ah, I have a question, any time do you apply ultrasound direct to the grafite solution? this man used this method 
Stankovich, S.; Dikin, D. A.; Piner, R. D.; Kohlhaas, K. A.; Kleinhammes, A.; Jia, Y.; Wu,
Y.; Nguyen, S. T.; Ruoff, R. S. (2007): Synthesis of graphene-based nanosheets via.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +aureliano buendia I have used that method too - the problem with it is that too long exposure to ultrasound splits the graphene sheets into fragments - i will read the paper you sent - thanks for that

author: @Pelukeitor
text:
lishowa urano  Ahora debes limpiar.
Deberá añadir dos litros de agua caliente. Dejar reposar. después de un día el compuesto estará en la parte inferior del recipiente. quitar el agua (con una jeringuilla grande por ejemplo, con cuidado de no tirar el fondo del recipiente) y agregar un litro de ácido clorhídrico débil a una solución de un molar. lavar con agua fría tres veces más y habrá terminado. el producto es el óxido de grafeno.
para conseguir esta disolucion en agua debe meterse en un baño ultrasonico(se usa para limpiar pulseras, anillos etc.).

PD: (El agua debe ser Destilada)

replies.author: @samuelencarnacion3364
replies.text:
